cast.voice_hint: group chat mom, runs on snacks and emotional labor, has everyone's allergies memorized
'it me' energy, relatable to a fault, slightly tired, showing up anyway
same energy as `<CHAR_B>`, but hasn't figured out how to show up yet

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Did you eat before this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I had some air.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: That's not—

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: And half a granola bar from my pocket.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I have a whole thing in my bag. Take the whole thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: There are so many people watching.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't look at them.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I can feel them looking at me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Look at me instead. I'm looking at you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay. Here's what we practiced.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I remember the thing we practiced.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Walk me through it anyway.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: So you can hear yourself. Got it. Very sneaky.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Hey. Um. Is this where we wait.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That is canonically where I also was standing ten minutes ago.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Oh no. Are we the same.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Lowkey yes. Lowkey don't panic about it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You can wait here. I have snacks.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: They just called my name and I think I left my body.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay so this is fine. This is totally fine. This is not fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Hey. Eyes on me. Both of you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I can't.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You can. You are. You're doing it right now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I didn't fall down.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Huge. That's huge.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Was anyone watching.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Everyone was watching. You were fine. No notes.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I had notes. All good ones.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: She does that. The good-notes thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Does it work.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Fr. It's actually kind of annoying how much it works.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You don't have to tell her my methods.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: She gets to know the methods. She's one of us now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: ...I'm one of you now?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You were always going to be. I just had to wait for you to show up.

state.conditioning_notes:
`<CHAR_A>`'s love of taking care of people and fear of not being needed shape every move — the snacks, the "look at me instead," the good-notes thing. Care is the method; being useful is the engine. The spike beat (narreme_005) is where this becomes most visible: `<CHAR_A>` doesn't freeze, doesn't comfort from a distance — they anchor two spiraling people simultaneously because that's exactly what they need to be doing. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of being understood and fear of being overlooked come through in the self-aware commentary: they narrate their own experience as a way of checking whether anyone's tracking it. The bond with `<CHAR_A>` is built on the fact that `<CHAR_A>` always is. `<CHAR_C>` shares `<CHAR_B>`'s core and immediately recognizes it — "are we the same" — which is the seed of connection. The arena location keeps everyone performing even in the waiting: they are watched even when they're not on yet, and every small gesture reads bigger under that pressure. The spike doesn't come from an external threat but from the pressure of being seen, which is exactly what the arena archetype produces.

➡️ end
